mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in industrial production signals improving manufacturing profitability and operational leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month change in INDPRO and S&P 500 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% reflects sustained restrictive monetary policy that elevates the risk-free rate and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and an elevated federal funds rate signal stagflationary pressure that depresses earnings expectations and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE is rising and FEDFUNDS is elevated.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales during a monetary tightening cycle signal overheating consumption that prompts additional rate hikes, increasing discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: During months with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% and RSAFS 3-month change exceeding 2%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening construction activity and downstream demand for materials and credit, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly acceleration in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month change in PAYEMS and S&P 500 5-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3.5% year-over-year forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, raising real discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY change exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and falling industrial production signals margin compression and a demand-supply imbalance that reduces earnings quality, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when RSAFS rises and INDPRO falls simultaneously.
